# Reliance Builder image generation (Nano Banana/Gemini) Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Every Reliance Builder build (all 5 categories) automatically gets a real, art-directed hero image generated by Google's Gemini image model ("Nano Banana"), with no manual "generate image" step.

**Architecture:** Claude's existing `plan` call authors four image-prompt parts (subject/action/location/framing) per `Conversation/ART_DIRECTION_RELIANCE_v4.md`'s rules; `App/src/ai/client.ts` deterministically assembles those parts plus a fixed "visual baseline" string (never authored by Claude) into the final prompt, then calls a new dev-server proxy (`App/geminiImageProxy.ts`) that forwards to Gemini's `generateContent` REST API and returns a data URL. The result is attached directly onto the existing `BuildPlan` object as `heroImage`, so it flows through the app's existing prop-threading (`App.tsx` → `ResultScreen` → `BuildPreview` → each preview component) with no new plumbing — only the 5 preview-renderer files need to read the new field.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Vite dev-server plugin, Gemini `generateContent` REST API (via `fetch`, no new npm dependency).

## Global Constraints

- `App/src/ai/fallbackPlan.ts` is not modified — the deterministic fallback plan never populates the new image fields, and `client.ts` must skip the image call entirely when they're absent (see Task 4).
- No changes to the `classify` Claude call or `CLASSIFY_TOOL` schema — only `plan` gets the art-direction system-prompt addition and the new schema fields.
- No new npm dependencies — call Gemini via `fetch`, matching this repo's existing minimal-deps convention and the same raw-`fetch` pattern `callAnthropic` in `App/aiServerPlugin.ts` already uses.
- `component_test` has no automated test runner (no vitest/jest) — verification is manual, via the running dev server, curl, and (where noted) the browser DevTools console — same approach used in prior plans for this project.
- Files under `.env*` patterns must not be Read or Edited by any task in this plan — this environment's permission settings hard-block it. The user has already added `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL=gemini-3.1-flash-image`, and `HIGGSFIELD_API_KEY` to the repo-root `.env` themselves; tasks only need to confirm the app can read them (e.g. via a successful API call), never inspect the file's contents directly.
- `heroImage` lives on `BuildPlan` itself (not a separate component prop) — do not modify `App/src/components/BuildPreview.tsx` or thread a new prop through `App.tsx`/`ResultScreen.tsx`; every preview component already receives the full `plan: BuildPlan` object and should read `plan.heroImage` directly.

---

### Task 1: Schema fields and the art-direction module

**Files:**
- Modify: `App/src/ai/schema.ts:26-44` (the `BuildPlan` interface)
- Create: `App/src/ai/artDirection.ts`

**Interfaces:**
- Produces: `BuildPlan` gains `imageSubject?: string`, `imageAction?: string`, `imageLocation?: string`, `imageFraming?: string`, `imageIsAerial?: boolean`, `imageColourNotes?: string`, `heroImage?: string`. Produces `RELIANCE_ART_DIRECTION: string`, `RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE: string`, `RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE_AERIAL: string` (containing the literal substring `{{colourNotes}}`) from `App/src/ai/artDirection.ts` — consumed by Task 2 (`RELIANCE_ART_DIRECTION`) and Task 4 (both baseline constants).

```ts
export interface BuildPlan {
  headline?: string;
  subheadline?: string;
  body?: string;
  kicker?: string;
  ctaLabel?: string;
  navItems?: string[];
  sections?: { title: string; body: string }[];
  screenTitle?: string;
  contentBlocks?: string[];
  socialFormat?: 'square' | 'story' | 'linkedin' | 'carousel';
  badgeLabel?: string;
  motionConcept?: 'loader' | 'transition' | 'intro-animation' | 'product-reveal' | 'micro-interaction';
  motionDescription?: string;
  dimensionVariant?: string;
  /**
   * Claude-authored image-prompt parts, per Conversation/ART_DIRECTION_RELIANCE_v4.md.
   * Assembled together with the fixed visual baseline by App/src/ai/client.ts — never
   * sent to Gemini as raw fields, and never authored by Claude as one combined string.
   */
  imageSubject?: string;
  imageAction?: string;
  imageLocation?: string;
  imageFraming?: string;
  imageIsAerial?: boolean;
  imageColourNotes?: string;
  /** The generated image (a data URL), attached by client.ts after a successful call to /api/gemini-image. Never authored by Claude. */
  heroImage?: string;
  /** Names the AI picked — always re-validated against the real registry before use. */
  recommendedComponentNames: string[];
  reasoning: string;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Create `App/src/ai/artDirection.ts`**

```ts
/**
 * Condensed Reliance visual-language guidance for Claude-authored image
 * prompts, adapted from Conversation/ART_DIRECTION_RELIANCE_v4.md. Appended
 * to the system prompt only for the `plan` call (see App/aiServerPlugin.ts) —
 * classify never authors image-prompt fields.
 */
export const RELIANCE_ART_DIRECTION = `
Image direction: Grounded Confidence. Every generated image expresses at least one of
three lenses — ideally all three: Authority & Respect (sharp geometric framing, precise
directional light, the subject has presence and mass); Scalability & Growth (the
environment implies scale beyond the frame — a worker against a vast solar field, a
single car that implies a city — the subject is never isolated); Care (hands doing
something real, location made specific, nothing decorative).

Apply the 7 principles: show people operating with real consequence (We Care); show the
moment value is delivered, visible not implied (Customer Value); sharp and considered,
never generic (Excellence); no hierarchy when multiple people appear (One Team); the
individual's work connects to something larger, the person is never small (Ownership
Mindset); every subject shot with the same precision regardless of role — eye level or
slight low angle, never looking down (Respect); never staged, never stock, real skin
tones and real light (Integrity).

Three rules that never break: (1) light always has a direction — sun low, one side,
long rich shadows, nothing flat; (2) the subject is bright with shadow detail
preserved — never crushed to black, never overexposed; (3) bokeh background depth with
intimate push-in framing — something soft anchors the foreground, the background
separates but does not disappear.

Framing: for people, medium close-up with a slight low angle, both hands doing
something specific (operating, tending, turning, connecting — never resting), subject
offset in frame, a soft out-of-focus foreground element. For machines/infrastructure/
aerials, true top-down or wide low angle (never tilted), wide enough to show scale,
light still rakes from one side even from above. Never: subject smiling at camera
unless explicitly asked, centred symmetrical composition, tilted aerial, empty
foreground with no anchor.

Location must be named with physical, specific detail (e.g. "red-brown Rajasthan
earth, dry scrubland, neem trees" — never "in India" or "typical Indian setting", which
produce nothing usable). Never write "hundreds" or "thousands" in an aerial shot — name
a real number or describe infrastructure extending to the horizon.

Never use: "dramatic lighting", "beautiful", "professional photography", "realistic",
"stunning", "perfect", "amazing", "high quality", "photorealistic" alone, "in India",
"Indian setting", "typical".

Absolute rule, no exceptions: never reference, name, imply, or visually include
anything associated with TATA Group — not the name, not a wordmark, not a
TATA-branded vehicle or product, not any logo or colour mark associated with TATA
Motors, TATA Power, TATA Steel, TCS, Jaguar Land Rover, or any TATA subsidiary.
Describe all vehicles/products/infrastructure generically: "white electric car", not a
brand name; "passenger vehicle", not a marque; "steel structure", not a manufacturer.

Author imageSubject (physical description + clothing), imageAction (both hands doing
something specific), imageLocation (named Indian place with physical detail), and
imageFraming (shot type and angle) as separate fields — do not include the visual
baseline in any of them, it is appended separately. Set imageIsAerial true only for a
genuine top-down/aerial shot, and when true, set imageColourNotes to the scene's
specific colours (e.g. "steel-blue panels and red-brown earth").
`.trim();

export const RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE =
  'eye level shot, warm golden directional light, bright on subject with rich shadow ' +
  'detail preserved, high colour saturation, bokeh background depth, intimate push-in ' +
  'framing, mid-to-high brightness, authentic Indian skin tones, vivid festive or ' +
  'natural colours, no artificial fill, cinematic documentary feel';

export const RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE_AERIAL =
  'true top-down aerial perspective, warm golden directional light raking across ' +
  'surfaces, high colour saturation, vivid {{colourNotes}} colours, mid-to-high ' +
  'brightness, no artificial fill, cinematic documentary feel';
```

### Task 3: Gemini image proxy

**Files:**
- Create: `App/geminiImageProxy.ts`
- Modify: `App/vite.config.ts`

**Interfaces:**
- Produces: `geminiImageProxy(): Plugin` (exported from `App/geminiImageProxy.ts`), registered as a Vite plugin in `App/vite.config.ts`. Exposes `POST /api/gemini-image` accepting `{ prompt: string }` and returning `{ result: { dataUrl: string } }` on success or `{ error: string }` (non-2xx) on failure — consumed by `App/src/ai/client.ts` in Task 4.

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `App/geminiImageProxy.ts`**

```ts
import type { Plugin, ViteDevServer } from 'vite';
import type { IncomingMessage, ServerResponse } from 'node:http';

/**
 * Dev-only local proxy to Google's Gemini image API ("Nano Banana").
 *
 * The browser NEVER sees GEMINI_API_KEY — it lives only in this Node
 * process (read from the repo-root .env, copied onto process.env by
 * App/vite.config.ts, same mechanism as aiServerPlugin.ts's Claude proxy).
 * The client posts { prompt: string } to /api/gemini-image; this middleware
 * forwards a single generateContent call requesting image output and
 * returns a data URL. If no key/model is configured, or the call fails for
 * any reason, it returns a JSON error the client already treats as "no
 * image" — App/src/ai/client.ts never throws on this, the preview just
 * renders without one.
 *
 * This only runs under `vite dev` (App/vite.config.ts), same constraint as
 * aiServerPlugin.ts's Claude proxy.
 */

const GEMINI_API_BASE = 'https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models';

interface ImageRequestBody {
  prompt: string;
}

interface GeminiGenerateContentResponse {
  candidates?: Array<{
    content?: {
      parts?: Array<{ inlineData?: { mimeType: string; data: string } }>;
    };
  }>;
}

function readJsonBody(req: IncomingMessage): Promise<ImageRequestBody> {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    let raw = '';
    req.on('data', (chunk) => (raw += chunk));
    req.on('end', () => {
      try {
        resolve(JSON.parse(raw));
      } catch (err) {
        reject(err);
      }
    });
    req.on('error', reject);
  });
}

function sendJson(res: ServerResponse, status: number, body: unknown) {
  res.statusCode = status;
  res.setHeader('content-type', 'application/json');
  res.end(JSON.stringify(body));
}

export function geminiImageProxy(): Plugin {
  return {
    name: 'reliance-builder-gemini-image-proxy',
    configureServer(server: ViteDevServer) {
      server.middlewares.use('/api/gemini-image', async (req, res) => {
        if (req.method !== 'POST') {
          sendJson(res, 405, { error: 'POST only' });
          return;
        }

        const apiKey = process.env.GEMINI_API_KEY;
        const model = process.env.GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL;
        if (!apiKey || !model) {
          sendJson(res, 503, {
            error: 'GEMINI_API_KEY or GEMINI_IMAGE_MODEL is not set. Add them to the repo-root .env.',
          });
          return;
        }

        try {
          const body = await readJsonBody(req);
          if (!body.prompt) {
            sendJson(res, 400, { error: 'Missing prompt' });
            return;
          }

          const apiRes = await fetch(`${GEMINI_API_BASE}/${model}:generateContent`, {
            method: 'POST',
            headers: {
              'content-type': 'application/json',
              'x-goog-api-key': apiKey,
            },
            body: JSON.stringify({
              contents: [{ parts: [{ text: body.prompt }] }],
              generationConfig: { responseModalities: ['IMAGE'] },
            }),
          });

          if (!apiRes.ok) {
            const text = await apiRes.text().catch(() => '');
            sendJson(res, 502, { error: `Gemini API error ${apiRes.status}: ${text.slice(0, 300)}` });
            return;
          }

          const data = (await apiRes.json()) as GeminiGenerateContentResponse;
          const part = data.candidates?.[0]?.content?.parts?.find((p) => p.inlineData);
          if (!part?.inlineData) {
            sendJson(res, 502, { error: 'Gemini response had no image data' });
            return;
          }

          sendJson(res, 200, { result: { dataUrl: `data:${part.inlineData.mimeType};base64,${part.inlineData.data}` } });
        } catch (err) {
          sendJson(res, 502, { error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown error calling Gemini' });
        }
      });
    },
  };
}
```

### Task 4: Client wiring — assemble the prompt and attach the generated image

**Files:**
- Modify: `App/src/ai/client.ts`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE`, `RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE_AERIAL` from `./artDirection` (Task 1); `POST /api/gemini-image` from Task 3.
- Produces: `requestPlan`'s returned `AIResult<BuildPlan>.data.heroImage` is populated (a data URL) whenever Claude authored all four image-prompt fields and the Gemini call succeeds; otherwise `undefined`. Consumed by the 5 preview components in Task 5 via `plan.heroImage`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the import**

In `App/src/ai/client.ts`, the existing import block starts with:

```ts
import type { BuildCategoryId, GuidedAnswers } from '../types';
import { BUILD_CATEGORIES } from '../data/buildCategories';
import { AVAILABLE_COMPONENTS } from '../data/oneuiRegistry';
import { fallbackClassify, fallbackPlan } from './fallbackPlan';
import type { AIResult, BuildPlan, ClassifyResult, FollowUpQuestion } from './schema';
```

Add one new line directly after it, without changing any of the existing lines:

```ts
import { RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE, RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE_AERIAL } from './artDirection';
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add the prompt-assembly and image-request helpers**

Add these two functions after `sanitizeFollowUps` and before `requestClassification`:

```ts
function assembleImagePrompt(plan: Pick<BuildPlan, 'imageSubject' | 'imageAction' | 'imageLocation' | 'imageFraming' | 'imageIsAerial' | 'imageColourNotes'>): string {
  const baseline = plan.imageIsAerial
    ? RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE_AERIAL.replace('{{colourNotes}}', plan.imageColourNotes || 'natural')
    : RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE;
  return [plan.imageSubject, plan.imageAction, plan.imageLocation, plan.imageFraming, baseline].join('\n');
}

async function requestHeroImage(plan: BuildPlan): Promise<string | undefined> {
  if (!plan.imageSubject || !plan.imageAction || !plan.imageLocation || !plan.imageFraming) return undefined;

  try {
    const res = await fetch('/api/gemini-image', {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: { 'content-type': 'application/json' },
      body: JSON.stringify({ prompt: assembleImagePrompt(plan) }),
    });
    const json = await res.json();
    if (!res.ok) return undefined;
    return typeof json.result?.dataUrl === 'string' ? json.result.dataUrl : undefined;
  } catch {
    return undefined;
  }
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Call it from `requestPlan`**

Replace:

```ts
  // Never trust component names from the model without cross-checking the real registry.
  const recommendedComponentNames = Array.isArray(raw.recommendedComponentNames)
    ? raw.recommendedComponentNames.filter((name) => AVAILABLE_COMPONENT_NAMES.has(name))
    : [];

  return {
    source: 'claude',
    data: {
      ...raw,
      recommendedComponentNames,
      reasoning: raw.reasoning || 'Authored by Claude.',
    },
  };
}
```

with:

```ts
  // Never trust component names from the model without cross-checking the real registry.
  const recommendedComponentNames = Array.isArray(raw.recommendedComponentNames)
    ? raw.recommendedComponentNames.filter((name) => AVAILABLE_COMPONENT_NAMES.has(name))
    : [];

  const data: BuildPlan = {
    ...raw,
    recommendedComponentNames,
    reasoning: raw.reasoning || 'Authored by Claude.',
  };
  data.heroImage = await requestHeroImage(data);

  return { source: 'claude', data };
}
```

Open the printed local URL in a browser, open DevTools console, and run:

```js

    .requestPlan({ category: 'website', prompt: 'a homepage for a new solar energy initiative', answers: {} })
    .then((r) => console.log(r.source, typeof r.data.heroImage, r.data.heroImage?.slice(0, 30))),
);
```

Expected: logs `claude object data:image/...` (the exact mime-type prefix may vary). If `typeof r.data.heroImage` is `'undefined'`, check the browser Network tab for the `/api/claude` and `/api/gemini-image` requests to see which step didn't produce what was expected, and report the specifics.

- [ ] **Step 5: Stop the dev server**

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit**

```bash
git add App/src/ai/client.ts
git commit -m "Wire client.ts to assemble the art-directed image prompt and attach the generated hero image"
```

---

### Task 5: Render the hero image in each preview

**Files:**
- Modify: `App/src/components/previews/WebsitePreview.tsx`
- Modify: `App/src/components/previews/AppScreenPreview.tsx`
- Modify: `App/src/components/previews/SlidePreview.tsx`
- Modify: `App/src/components/previews/SocialPreview.tsx`
- Modify: `App/src/components/previews/MotionPreview.tsx`

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes: `plan.heroImage: string | undefined` (Task 4) — already available on the `plan: BuildPlan` prop every one of these components already receives. No new props are added to any of these components, and `App/src/components/BuildPreview.tsx` is not modified (per Global Constraints).
- Uses the real `Image` component from `@jds4/oneui-react` (`src`, `alt`, `aspectRatio`, `width` props — confirmed via `node_modules/@jds4/oneui-react/dist/components/Image/Image.shared.d.ts`), matching this app's existing convention of using real OneUI components rather than raw `<img>` tags.

- [ ] **Step 6: Verify visually**

Run (from `/Users/keithbone/component_test`), in the background: `npm run app:dev`
Open the printed local URL in a browser and submit a build prompt for each of the 5 categories in turn (website, app-screens, slides, social-media, motion — the start screen has quick-action buttons per category). For each, confirm an image renders in the preview once the build finishes (this may take a few seconds longer than before, since it now waits on both the Claude and Gemini calls). If you cannot drive an actual browser in this environment, instead use the DevTools-console check from Task 4 Step 4 for each category (changing `category` in the `requestPlan` call), and additionally confirm via `document.querySelector('img[src^="data:image"]')` in the console after triggering a build through the UI that an `<img>` with a data URL is actually present in the rendered DOM — this proves the component tree renders it, not just that `client.ts` returns it.

```bash
git commit -m "Render the generated hero image in every preview category"
```

---

## Self-Review Notes

- **Spec coverage:** Task 1 covers spec §1 (schema) and half of §3 (art-direction
  module); Task 2 covers spec §2 (aiServerPlugin.ts); Task 3 covers spec §4 (the Gemini
  proxy); Task 4 covers spec §5 (client wiring); Task 5 covers spec §6 (preview
  integration). One deliberate implementation simplification versus the spec's exact
  wording: §6 said preview components "gain a `heroImage` prop" — since `heroImage`
  ends up living directly on `BuildPlan` (Task 1), and every preview component already
  receives the full `plan` object, no separate prop or `BuildPreview.tsx` change is
  needed; this is called out explicitly in Global Constraints so no task reintroduces
  it.
- **No placeholders:** every step has literal code, exact file diffs, and concrete
  curl/console verification commands with expected output.
- **Type consistency:** `imageSubject`/`imageAction`/`imageLocation`/`imageFraming`/
  `imageIsAerial`/`imageColourNotes`/`heroImage` are defined once in Task 1's
  `BuildPlan` and referenced by those exact names in Tasks 2 (JSON schema keys), 4
  (destructured/assigned in `client.ts`), and 5 (`plan.heroImage` reads). `RELIANCE_ART_DIRECTION` is defined once in Task 1 and imported by that exact name in Task 2; `RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE`/`RELIANCE_VISUAL_BASELINE_AERIAL` are defined once in Task 1 and imported by those exact names in Task 4.
- **Known risk flagged for implementation:** the exact Gemini `generateContent` request
  shape for image output (`generationConfig.responseModalities: ['IMAGE']`) and the
  `x-goog-api-key` header are based on documented conventions but unverified live in
  this environment — Task 3 Step 3's verification will either confirm it or surface the
  real error text to adjust from, rather than the plan silently assuming success.
